ogTitle: Cookernup vs Cooladar Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cookernup and Cooladar Hill.
heading: Cookernup vs Cooladar Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cookernup, Western Australia and Cooladar Hill, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cooladar Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Cookernup (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cookernup or Cooladar Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cookernup has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Cooladar Hill scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cookernup scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Cooladar Hill scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cookernup or Cooladar Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cooladar Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
